name: ai-daily-digest
author: vigorX777

metadata.name: ai-daily-digest
metadata.description: AI-curated daily tech digest from 90 top Hacker News blogs with bilingual support
metadata.author: vigorX777

features: 90 curated tech blog sources from Andrej Karpathy's HN recommendations
AI-powered multi-dimensional article scoring (AI/ML, Security, Engineering, Tools, Opinion, Other)
Bilingual output support (Chinese and English via --lang flag)
Smart filtering with configurable article count and time range
Visual statistics with Mermaid charts and ASCII visualizations
Zero dependency single TypeScript file using Bun runtime
Configuration persistence in ~/.hn-daily-digest/config.json
Dual AI provider support (Gemini primary, OpenAI fallback)
Markdown digest format with Top 3 previews and trend analysis
Concurrent RSS fetching from all 90 sources
notes: ⚠️ VERY YOUNG REPOSITORY - Created Feb 14, 2026 (3 weeks old)
⚠️ NO LICENSE FILE - Legal status unclear, use at own risk
⚠️ Minimal maintenance - Only 7 commits total
⭐ 1,400 GitHub stars - Good community interest despite young age
💰 API COSTS - Heavy usage: ~90 RSS feeds + AI scoring per run
🔧 Requires Bun runtime installation (not standard Node.js)
🌐 Bilingual capable - Use --lang en for English, --lang zh for Chinese
📚 Quality sources - Uses Andrej Karpathy's curated HN blog list
📝 Chinese documentation - README and SKILL.md entirely in Chinese
💾 Smart defaults - 48 hours lookback, 15 articles, saves preferences
⚠️ Use with caution - Very new, no license, unclear long-term support
Best for: Daily tech news consumers comfortable with Bun and API costs
NOT for: Production use, license-sensitive environments, budget users

name: confidence-check
author: Cepheid (extracted from SuperClaude)

metadata.name: confidence-check
metadata.description: Assess task confidence before starting to save 5k-50k tokens by preventing wrong directions
metadata.author: Cepheid

features: Three-tier confidence system (≥90% proceed, 70-89% investigate, <70% ask questions)
Token efficiency: 100-200 token investment saves 5,000-50,000 tokens
Systematic evaluation of task clarity and feasibility
Documentation of assumptions before implementation
Red flag detection for low-confidence indicators
Integration with investigation and clarification workflows
Evidence-based confidence assessment framework
notes: 🏆 Extracted from SuperClaude Framework's Confidence-First Implementation pattern
💡 ROI: 25x to 250x in token savings by catching wrong directions early
⚡ Prevents wasted work on misunderstood requirements
📋 Works best with evidence-based-dev for investigation phase
🎯 Complements TDD workflow and code review processes
✅ Simple to adopt: Just ask yourself confidence level before starting
Best for: Complex tasks, unfamiliar tech, unclear requirements
NOT for: Trivial changes, well-understood routine tasks

name: self-check-protocol
author: Cepheid (extracted from SuperClaude)

metadata.name: self-check-protocol
metadata.description: Systematic validation framework with 4 questions and 7 red flags to ensure genuine task completion
metadata.author: Cepheid

features: Four fundamental validation questions (tests passing, requirements met, assumptions verified, evidence provided)
Seven red flags detecting common implementation errors
Prevents false completion claims (tests passing without output, partial implementations)
Assumption verification framework
Evidence-based validation for all claims
Integration with TDD and code review workflows
Quick reference card for easy adoption
notes: 🏆 Extracted from SuperClaude Framework's SelfCheckProtocol
🔍 Catches errors that passing tests might miss
⚠️ Critical for preventing 'works but wrong' implementations
🧪 Essential companion to TDD workflow
📚 Complements confidence-check and evidence-based-dev
✅ Systematic approach prevents rushing to completion
Best for: All implementations before marking complete
Use after: Tests pass but before claiming done

name: wave-execution
author: Cepheid (extracted from SuperClaude)

metadata.name: wave-execution
metadata.description: Parallel execution pattern achieving 3.5x speedup through Wave→Checkpoint→Wave workflow
metadata.author: Cepheid

features: Wave→Checkpoint→Wave parallel execution pattern
3.5x faster than sequential execution
Identify and execute independent operations concurrently
Collective analysis at checkpoints for pattern recognition
Multiple tool calls in single message for true parallelism
Examples for file operations, searches, and tests
Decision matrix for when to use wave execution
notes: 🏆 Extracted from SuperClaude Framework's Wave-Checkpoint-Wave pattern
⚡ Performance gain: 3.5x to 6x faster than sequential
🎯 Perfect for multi-file operations, searches, and batch processing
💡 Key insight: Checkpoint analyzes ALL results collectively, not individually
⚠️ Only use when operations are truly independent
📊 Includes performance calculation formulas
Best for: Multi-file refactoring, parallel tests, batch operations
NOT for: Operations with dependencies, atomic transactions

name: evidence-based-dev
author: Cepheid (extracted from SuperClaude)

metadata.name: evidence-based-dev
metadata.description: Never assume, always verify - back every decision with evidence from official sources or tests
metadata.author: Cepheid

features: Never Assume → Always Verify principle
Five-tier evidence source reliability ranking
Systematic assumption identification and verification
Evidence documentation templates
Red flag detection for assumption-based language
Verification techniques (documentation, code archaeology, experiments, TDD)
Evidence trail for decisions and claims
notes: 🏆 Extracted from SuperClaude Framework's Evidence-Based Development principle
📚 Tier 1 evidence: Official docs, API specs, source code
🔍 Tier 2 evidence: Existing patterns, tests, configuration
🧪 Tier 3 evidence: Experiments, benchmarks
⚠️ Never trust assumptions or Stack Overflow without verification
📊 Includes evidence source reliability rankings
🎯 Essential for confidence-check investigation phase
Best for: Unfamiliar APIs, architectural decisions, performance claims
Critical for: Production code, security features, public APIs
